comments.text: Berber is a pejorative and outdated word used by greeks and romans to describe North Africa because they suffered heavy losses there. The preferred term nowadays is Amazigh.

comments.author: @redghost777
comments.text: My husband and i went to Morocco 11 years ago. Very interesting country. Very dirty streets in the city. The country side was clean and beautiful.  I highly recommend visiting.

replies.author: @jameshenning7105
replies.text: So the only thing you remembered is the dirty streets. comments.author: @SilentWanderYT-TV
comments.text: Moroccan cuisine is celebrated for its perfect balance of savory and sweet. Meals like tagine (slow-cooked stew) and couscous, heavily spiced with cumin and saffron and sweetened with ingredients like honey and dried fruits, are staples. It is also culturally customary to be welcomed with a glass of sweetened mint tea.
